# input4j

> Lightweight, cross-platform Java library for unified gamepad and joystick input handling.

input4j is a pure Java input library that provides a unified API for gamepad, joystick, and controller input across Windows, Linux, and macOS. It uses the Foreign Function & Memory API (Java 22+) and requires zero native dependencies.

## Key Pages

- [Home](https://gurkenlabs.github.io/input4j/): Project overview, quick start guide, and feature highlights
- [Documentation](https://gurkenlabs.github.io/input4j/docs): Complete usage documentation
- [Cross-Platform Java Input](https://gurkenlabs.github.io/input4j/docs/cross-platform-java-input): FFM API guide for native input without JNI
- [Java Game Integration](https://gurkenlabs.github.io/input4j/docs/java-game-integration): Integrating input4j into Java games
- [FFM API Architecture](https://gurkenlabs.github.io/input4j/docs/architecture-ffm-api): Internal architecture and platform implementations
- [LITIENGINE Case Study](https://gurkenlabs.github.io/input4j/docs/litiengine-input4j-migration): Real-world migration from JInput

## Technical Details

- **Language**: Java 22+
- **Platforms**: Windows (XInput, DirectInput), Linux (evdev), macOS (IOKit HID)
- **Dependencies**: None (pure Java via FFM API)
- **License**: MIT
- **Maven**: `de.gurkenlabs:input4j:1.1.1`
- **Source**: https://github.com/gurkenlabs/input4j
